title: Juglans × quadrangulata
intro: Juglans × quadrangulata, also known as butternut, is a hybrid plant species within the walnut family (Juglandaceae) that is native to eastern North America. The health benefits of butternut include its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, which can help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. The bioactive constituents of butternut include juglone, a phenolic compound with potential antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, as well as other polyphenolic compounds such as flavonoids and tannins. Butternut is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are valued for their potential to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Juglans × quadrangulata is a hybrid of the black walnut (Juglans nigra) and the eastern black walnut (Juglans cinerea). This hybrid is a result of human cultivation and is not found in the wild. As it's a man-made cross between two walnut species, it does not have a natural classification in the botanical sense. The classification of Juglans × quadrangulata is often considered as a cultivar or a hybrid species. Its classification is mainly based on its characteristics and genetic makeup.
uses: Juglans × quadrangulata is a hybrid plant that is a cross between Juglans cinerea (butternut) and Juglans nigra (black walnut). Juglans × quadrangulata has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. Its bark, leaves, and hulls are used for medicinal purposes, with reported uses including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant has been used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and digestive issues, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Further studies are required to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential benefits.

preparations: Juglans × quadrangulata is a hybrid of walnut trees, and its herbal preparations are primarily used in traditional medicine for their potential health benefits.
